notes: Lesson 14 orientation (act 80), KV Cache / serving with a KV cache demo lesson, 1004.88s audio, 7 route-aligned content cues re-placed from the real spiral overview transcript (High-level map / Analogy / Tiny example / Mechanism / Implementation / Misconception / Synthesis), specialized to the actual PREFILL-DECODE-KV-CACHE content and reusing the transformer.py idiom lib (token_row, hidden_matrix, block_pipeline) plus local KV-cache grid helpers. Running example 'The cat sat' -> prefill files a K,V row per token -> decode computes one query and attends over the cache -> append a row per decoded token. Two review iterations. Round-1 (author): smoke-rendered every cue final frame with manim -s (fast safe-area/import guard) and caught+fixed 4 layout issues before any long render: tokens overran the left safe edge, three bottom closing-notes overran the bottom safe area, and Cue03 cost labels collided -> retuned widths/x and note buffs. Round-2 (this pass): full -ql render (7 chunks, 854x480x15, niced/serial) + READ start/mid/end for all 7 cues; all cues pass with no further defects. Per-cue checklist (formula/label legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches the beat narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Note: cue03/cue04/cue05 'end' frames are sampled exactly at the cue boundary with fast keyframe seeking and show the next cue's opening title (documented cumulative chunk-pinning artifact, same as Lessons 12/13); each cue's true final state was re-verified by accurate-seek extraction from silent_concat (cue03 settled @571.5s shows the O(n^2) vs O(n) cost contrast; cue04 settled @715s shows the M=L*2*n*d legend; cue05's settled 4-mix-up grid is fully shown in its mid frame; cue06 end re-extracted @1004.3s shows the settled synthesis with 5 perspective cards). Final -ql 1004.738s vs audio 1004.88s (delta 0.142s, within tolerance). Per-cue: Cue0: serving has two phases, prefill reads the whole prompt once then decode emits one new token at a time; PREFILL -> DECODE map with The/cat/sat tokens and a KV cache grid (one row per token, every layer) that grows +1 row per generated token -> long chats cost memory. | Cue1: the serving pipeline as a workshop line: the prompt -> Prefill(read the whole prompt) -> File K,V(one receipt per token) -> Decode(reuse the filed receipts) -> Append(one new receipt/token); tags inference/kv-cache/serving name the stations. | Cue2: prefill files a K and V row per token; The/cat/sat each route to a key-cache row (amber) and a value-cache row (teal); attend over the cache, never re-read the raw prompt. | Cue3: decode computes one query q (the ONLY new vector this step) and attends over the cached keys (reused) via attn = softmax(q K^T / sqrt(d)) V; without cache you recompute K,V for all n -> O(n^2), with cache you compute 1 new K,V -> O(n). Math verified. | Cue4: a growing per-layer tensor cache_k and cache_v, shape [sequence x dimension], with memory M = L * 2 * n * d and legend (L layers, 2 keys & values, n sequence length, d hidden dimension); append a row per step, attend over the whole cache. | Cue5: four tempting mix-ups as A != B (prefill != decode, a cached K/V != the final answer, saving compute != saving memory, a phase name != the operation), revealed one at a time; reuse skips recompute but the cache still grows with context. | Cue6: prefill builds it, decode reuses and grows it: the loop prefill -> KV cache -> decode -> +1 token with a curved append-back arrow ('append the token's K,V -> cache grows -> decode again'); the cache grows one row per decoded token (5 rows +'the' -> 6 rows +'mat') and five perspective cards (Audio/Visual/Text/Code/Assess) light one at a time.
